blocks.content: The funeral was on a Saturday.
Small, the way Ruth had wanted it. Millhaven Baptist, the windows full of April light.
He'd written it the night before. Daisy had stayed up with him, sitting nearby.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: My mother taught me that showing up is a form of love. That staying is a form of love. She never said that in words — she just did it, every day, for twenty years, in a house that was quieter than it should have been. She showed me that love is not what you feel when things are easy. It's what you do when things are hard. I learned that from watching her. I hope I've been worth what she gave.
In the front row, Daisy kept her eyes on him.
After the service, people moved through the fellowship hall.
By late afternoon the house was quiet again. Kit and Daisy sat on the back porch in the cool evening and didn't say much.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She was so proud of you.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Not just the film. All of it. You.
He nodded. Looked out at the yard.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I want to stay a few more days.
He put his arm around her. She leaned in. The yard went dark.
